name: Anti-uPAR CAR T improved aged-mouse metabolic and fitness endpoints
summary: A 2024 uPAR CAR T study supplies a direct physiological-aging mouse signal for exercise capacity and metabolic dysfunction.
statement: Anti-uPAR CAR T cells safely targeted uPAR-positive senescent cells in aged mice, improved exercise capacity during physiological aging, and improved metabolic dysfunction including glucose tolerance in aged and high-fat-diet mice.
time_horizon: Single-administration, long-term therapeutic and preventive mouse effects
quantitative_note: The source reports that a single administration was sufficient for long-term therapeutic and preventive effects in mouse models.
caveats: No treated-human aging result is available.
Exercise capacity and glucose tolerance in mice do not establish human functional benefit.
The source discloses patent and advisory interests related to senolytic CAR T cells.
